title: Fence .atm history mutation and staged ownership so weak agents cannot self-clean other lanes
acceptance: Attempts to unstage or remove another task's staged ATM evidence are blocked with a deterministic diagnostic.
Same-task governed evidence writes still succeed through the approved close / claim / release paths.
Protected-surface checks are shared rather than duplicated across hook, taskflow, and git-governance flows.
Any new script or helper introduced by this card stays under 600 lines.
outOfScope: Changing the normal same-task close flow
nonGoals: Do not solve ambiguity by deleting or unstaging files automatically
rollbackNotes: Revert if legitimate same-task evidence writes or governed closeback commits become impossible.
notes:
- Reuse the staged ownership concepts already present in CID and hook-era work;
  harden the fence rather than inventing a second ownership vocabulary.
- This card is about stronger boundaries and better operator feedback, not
  about automatically repairing damaged runtime files.
